title: Freedom Day Finally Arrives
story: Every June 19, Americans celebrate Juneteenth, the day that marks when enslaved people in Texas finally learned they were free in 1865. Today, people honor this important holiday with parades, barbecues, and festivals filled with music, dancing, and delicious red foods like strawberry cake and red velvet cupcakes. title: Jamaican Guitar Legend Strums to Life
story: A musical star was born on June 19, 1932. Ernest Ranglin, the Jamaican guitar wizard, entered the world in Manchester, Jamaica. His nimble fingers would later create magic at Studio One and alongside reggae icon Jimmy Cliff. Ranglin's unique style helped shape the sound of Jamaican music, blending jazz, ska, and reggae into a vibrant musical tapestry.
activity_question: Where was Ernest Ranglin born?
follow_up_question: What styles of music did Ernest Ranglin blend in his playing?

title: The Birth of a Comic Book Legend
story: Pow! Zap! In 1915, a future superhero of the comic book world was born. Julius Schwartz entered the scene on June 19 in The Bronx, New York. He would grow up to become a powerhouse editor at DC Comics, shaping the adventures of iconic characters that kids still love today.
activity_question: Where was Julius Schwartz born?
follow_up_question: Can you name a famous superhero published by DC Comics?

title: Bluegrass Legend Strums into History
story: Lester Flatt, a guitar-picking pioneer, was born in Duncan's Chapel, Tennessee in 1914. His fingers would later create the twangy magic of bluegrass music. Flatt teamed up with Earl Scruggs, forming the famous duo Flatt & Scruggs. They strummed their way to fame with tunes like 'The Ballad of Jed Clampett' and 'Foggy Mountain Breakdown'. On June 19, bluegrass gained a true star.
activity_question: What famous musical duo did Lester Flatt form?
bonus_fact: Bluegrass music got its name from Bill Monroe's band, 'The Blue Grass Boys'.

title: Pioneering Pharmacologist's Birthday
story: Karl H. Beyer Jr. was born in Henderson, Kentucky in 1914. This brilliant pharmacologist would go on to make groundbreaking discoveries. Beyer's work led to the creation of the first medicines to treat high blood pressure and gout. His inventions helped countless people live healthier lives.
activity_question: What two medical conditions did Karl H. Beyer Jr. help create treatments for?
follow_up_question: Why do you think discovering new medicines is important?
